title: fragaria elatior var. muricata
intro: Fragaria elatior var. muricata, also known as the woodland strawberry or creeping strawberry, is a variety of the wild strawberry plant that is native to Europe and Asia. This plant is used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to stimulate digestion, relieve digestive issues, and act as an anti-inflammatory agent. The health benefits of Fragaria elatior var. muricata include its potential to support gut health, reduce inflammation, and boost the immune system. Herbal preparations of this plant may include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to support digestive health and overall well-being.
